features.title: Form Submit Hydration Fix
features.description: Convert the three P3-01/02/03 form components from <form onSubmit> + <button type="submit"> to <button type="button" onClick> + Enter-key handling, so the browser does not natively submit the form as a GET before React hydrates. This unblocks the P3-04 E2E in Next.js dev mode and removes the latent UX hazard of passwords in URL query params.
acceptance_criteria: Feature only modifies the three form components in apps/web/src/components/.
No backend, API, database, schema, migration, authorization, or E2E spec changes are required.
login-form.tsx submit button uses type=button and onClick={handleSubmit} (or an equivalent wrapper).
create-goal-space-form.tsx submit button uses type=button and onClick={handleSubmit} (or an equivalent wrapper).
create-node-board-form.tsx submit button uses type=button and onClick={handleSubmit} (or an equivalent wrapper).
All three forms keep <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}> so Enter-to-submit continues to work after hydration.
pnpm --filter @keplar/web lint exits 0 with no new warnings.
pnpm --filter @keplar/web test exits 0; all three focused UI test files pass without modification.
pnpm --filter @keplar/web e2e exits 0 with 1 passed for the P3-04 happy-path spec.
dependencies: P3-04 happy-path spec (1e9d03f) which depends on these forms submitting reliably
test_needs: Three focused UI test files (no modifications expected)
Playwright happy-path E2E run via pnpm --filter @keplar/web e2e
Requested lint, typecheck, format, and diff checks
definition_of_done: Required tests pass or documented exceptions are recorded
Required verification matrix items pass or have documented exceptions
